mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate over three months signals a sharp economic contraction is underway. This negatively impacts expected corporate earnings and investor sentiment, driving equity prices down over the subsequent quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment rate has been rising steadily since mid-2007
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where the unemployment rate rises more than 0.5pp in a 3-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Aggressive cuts to the federal funds rate by the central bank reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings and signal policy support for the economy, boosting equity valuations over the short term.
evidence_refs: fact:The Federal Reserve has cut the Fed Funds rate multiple times since September 2007.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in FEDFUNDS has a statistically significant positive correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When aggressive monetary easing (falling FEDFUNDS) coincides with a sharp contraction in housing starts (falling HOUST), it signals that the central bank is reacting to a severe underlying economic crisis centered on the housing market. This combination indicates high systemic risk, leading to negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Ongoing subprime mortgage crisis and collapse of Bear Stearns in March 2008.
failure_condition: Instances where both FEDFUNDS and HOUST are falling sharply are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing and business activity. This directly translates to lower corporate revenues and earnings, causing investors to revise expectations downward and sell equities.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production has shown a clear decelerating trend since late 2007.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in INDPRO has a statistically insignificant or positive correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over the full sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation coinciding with a weakening labor market (rising UNRATE) creates a stagflationary environment. This constrains the central bank's ability to ease policy aggressively and erodes corporate profit margins, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY > 3.5% AND UNRATE YoY change > 0.5pp
evidence_refs: fact:Commentary reflects rising concerns about stagflation in early 2008.
failure_condition: Periods meeting the stagflation trigger are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns on average.

mechanism_narrative: Negative monthly growth in nonfarm payrolls is a primary coincident indicator of recession. This signals falling aggregate income and consumption, leading to immediate downward revisions of earnings forecasts and negative equity market returns.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS has printed negative for several consecutive months in early 2008.
failure_condition: Months with negative payrolls growth are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month decline in real retail sales indicates an abrupt slowdown in consumer spending. This signals imminent downward revisions to revenue forecasts for consumer-facing companies, prompting short-term equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: Instances of real retail sales falling more than 0.5% month-over-month are not followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns on average.

mechanism_narrative: A negative quarter-over-quarter growth rate in real GDP is a definitive signal that the economy is in recession. This confirmation of broad-based economic weakness reduces long-term earnings growth expectations across the market, leading to a sustained decline in equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:Advance estimate for Q1 2008 GDP was very weak, signaling a potential stall.
failure_condition: Quarters with negative real GDP growth are followed by positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns.
